fact: A magnitude 9.1 earthquake ruptured the seafloor
source: U.S. Geological Survey — Magnitude 9.1 Sumatra-Andaman Islands Earthquake FAQ

fact: The tsunami moved at the speed of a jet across open ocean
source: NOAA Center for Tsunami Research — Tsunami waves in deep ocean can travel at speeds up to 500-600 mph

fact: The Indian Ocean had no tsunami warning system in 2004
source: UNESCO Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission — Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (established after 2004)

fact: The wave reached approximately three stories (around 30 meters in some locations)
source: USGS Professional Paper 1707: The Sumatra-Andaman Islands Earthquake and Tsunami of December 26, 2004 — wave heights up to 30 m recorded in Banda Aceh

fact: Approximately 230,000 people died across fourteen countries
source: USGS — death toll estimates of approximately 227,898–230,000+ across 14 countries; widely documented figure

fact: The ocean receded hundreds of meters from the beach before the wave arrived, and many people walked onto the exposed seafloor
source: Widely documented in survivor accounts and post-disaster analyses; see National Geographic coverage and NOAA educational materials on tsunami drawback
